critic.verdict: The chart has good structure and song mapping, but contains major difficulty spikes (8th notes directly connected to jumps) and highly syncopated rhythms that are inappropriate for an Easy chart.

issues.problem: An 8th note tap on U immediately following a JUMP LR is a massive difficulty spike that is too physically demanding for an Easy chart.
Extended syncopated sequence using dotted-quarter rhythms (off-beats) is highly confusing for beginners and should be simplified to steady quarter notes.
An 8th note transition from a tap U directly into a JUMP LR is too fast and mechanically complex for players at this level.
An isolated 8th note tap here creates an unnecessary difficulty spike during the outro.

critic.verdict: An incredibly rhythmic and catchy chart that captures the bounce of the track perfectly, though marred by a single invalid 3-panel jump.

issues.problem: The JUMP LR at 23.532 occurs while the D panel is held (HOLD D until 23.878), creating an unintentional 3-panel 'hand' requirement that breaks single-play pad conventions.
The 16th-note transition from tap D (42.805) directly into JUMP LR (42.920) forces an awkward foot recovery or double-step on the right foot.
Isolated 16th doublets feel slightly disconnected from the surrounding rhythmic density and can catch players off-guard.

critic.verdict: A highly rhythmic and engaging chart with excellent clap-sync, let down slightly by a few awkward same-foot 16th sequences during holds.

issues.problem: Forced 16th-note same-foot transition (D -> L) in 115ms while holding U, which is highly awkward and disrupts natural alternate flow.
Repeats the forced 16th same-foot slide/jack (D -> L) during a U hold, causing physical discomfort or requiring advanced bracket techniques.
The transition from a 16th-note tap R to JUMP LD is very sudden and feels slightly jerky to perform.
